content.problemStatement:
For a positive, even\textbf{even}even integer NNN, we call a pair of arrays AAA and BBB to be **interesting** if they satisfy the following conditions :



∣A∣=∣B∣=N/2|A| = |B| = N/2∣A∣=∣B∣=N/2 i.e. the length of array AAA is equal to the length of array BBB.




Each integer from 111 to NNN occurs exactly once in exactly one of the arrays.




The ithi^{th}ith prefix sum of AAA is not equal to ithi^{th}ith prefix sum of BBB for all 1≤i≤N/2−11 \leq i \leq N/2-11≤i≤N/2−1. 

Formally, ∑j=1iAj\displaystyle \sum_{j = 1}^{i} A_jj=1∑i​Aj​ != ∑j=1iBj \displaystyle \sum_{j = 1}^{i} B_jj=1∑i​Bj​ for all 1≤i≤N/2−11 \leq i \leq N/2 - 11≤i≤N/2−1




Sum of all elements in AAA is equal to sum of all elements in BBB i.e. ∑j=1N/2Aj=∑j=1N/2Bj\displaystyle \sum_{j = 1}^{N/2} A_j = \sum_{j = 1}^{N/2} B_jj=1∑N/2​Aj​=j=1∑N/2​Bj​



You are given a positive, even integer NNN. If there exists an **interesting** pair of arrays, then print "YES" followed by an interesting pair for this given NNN. If there exists multiple **interesting** pairs of arrays for given NNN, you can print any. Print "NO" in a single line if no such pair exists.


Input Format

First line of input will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, the integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, if there exists an **interesting** pair of arrays, say (A,B)(A, B)(A,B), then in the first line print "YES", in the second line print array AAA separated by spaces, and in third line print array BBB separated by spaces. Print "NO" in a single line if no such pair exists. If there are multiple answers, print any of them.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T lines follow, each containing a single even integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, if an interesting pair of arrays does not exist print a line with "NO". Otherwise print three lines: first line "YES", second line the N/2 integers of array A separated by spaces, third line the N/2 integers of array B separated by spaces.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 2: Consider A=[1,4]A = [1, 4]A=[1,4] and B=[2,3]B = [2, 3]B=[2,3]. Every integer from 111 to 444 occurs exactly once in exactly one of the arrays. Also, 111st prefix sum of AAA is not equal to 111st prefix sum of BBB (1≠21 \neq 21=2). And sum of the elements is equal to 555 for both arrays. So, (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) is an interesting pair.

solution.approach:
The total sum of numbers 1..N is N(N+1)/2. For two arrays of equal size N/2 to have the same sum, each must sum to N(N+1)/4, therefore N(N+1) must be divisible by 4. For even N this happens exactly when N % 4 == 0. If N % 4 != 0 we output NO.

When N % 4 == 0 we construct the arrays as follows. Let k = N/4.
- For i = 1 .. k: put i into A and i + N/2 into B.
- For i = k+1 .. N/2: put i + N/2 into A and i into B.
Both arrays contain N/2 distinct numbers, together they use every integer from 1 to N exactly once, and each array sums to N(N+1)/4.

Prefix sums differ for every i < N/2: for the first k positions B’s elements are always larger than A’s, so the prefix sum of B is larger. After position k the roles are swapped, but the accumulated advantage of B from the first part never disappears, therefore the two prefix sums can never become equal before the final position where they both equal the total sum.

The construction is O(N) per test case and uses O(N) additional memory for the output strings.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, overall O( sum of N over all test cases ) ≤ 10^6.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the two arrays of each test case.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        if N % 4 != 0:
            out_lines.append("NO")
            continue
        out_lines.append("YES")
        half = N // 2
        k = N // 4
        A = []
        B = []
        for i in range(1, half + 1):
            if i <= k:
                A.append(i)
                B.append(i + half)
            else:
                A.append(i + half)
                B.append(i)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, A)))
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, B)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
